payload.description: CHAR_A: "This is not where I saw my afternoon going."

cast.voice_hint: 'It me' energy. Relatable to a fault, self-aware, slightly tired, showing up anyway.
The earnest one. Roots for everyone unironically. Caring out loud is actually cool.
Knows a guy. Shows up and things start happening.

location.notes: constrained, hard to exit; locked room, cave, stuck elevator

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: This is not where I saw my afternoon going.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Same. Very same.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't do that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Do what.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Agree with me. We're not agreeing right now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: There is enough space if you move two steps left.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I am already two steps left.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Then the space situation is just what it is.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah. It is. No cap, this is a lot.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay. I know a thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Is the thing a way out.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The thing is adjacent to a way out.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That is so much worse than just saying no.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: If you two hold that side, I can make this work.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You sure.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Seventy percent.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I respect the honesty. Fine. Moving.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Same.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You actually believed it was going to work.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Yeah. I do that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Doesn't that get exhausting.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Doesn't not doing it get exhausting.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...That was a genuinely good point. I'm annoyed.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It worked. By the way. The thing worked.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're kidding.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I don't kid about adjacency.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Told you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You literally said nothing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I believed it quietly. Same thing.

state.conditioning_notes:
`<CHAR_A>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked surface in how they resist agreement — if `<CHAR_B>` agrees too easily, it reads as not actually seeing them. The rival dynamic is less "enemies" and more "two people who remind each other of the gap between them." `<CHAR_B>`'s love of believing in people and fear of being made to feel naive shape the "doesn't not doing it get exhausting" moment — they don't defend the belief, they just quietly live it, and that lands harder. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of making things happen and fear of being all talk drive the "adjacent to a way out" hedge — they need to under-promise so delivery still counts. The Trap location does not appear as description; it shows up in the stillness, the territorial step-counting, the stale air, and the fact that all three characters have nothing to do but wait and talk. Tension starts at 3 (friction, not threat), falls through reluctant proximity into actual connection, and bottoms out at 1 with the rivals quietly, accidentally, impressed by each other.

➡️ end
